task_completion.description: Open the inventory ODS file in a spreadsheet application
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is visible with the home inventory data loaded, showing columns like item name, category, purchase date, and price.
The 'Category' column contains only the standard categories: Electronics, Furniture, Appliances, Jewelry, or Tools. No messy, misspelled, or inconsistent category names are visible.
The date column displays dates in a uniform, consistent format (e.g., all YYYY-MM-DD or all MM/DD/YYYY) across all visible rows.

A column related to depreciation or current value is visible and populated with calculated numbers. The values should reflect category-specific depreciation (e.g., Jewelry items should show no depreciation).
Items with a purchase price greater than $1000 have a visible flag, such as a 'Yes' in a 'Needs Documentation' column, a specific cell highlight color, or a text marker.

task_completion.description: Apply consistent formatting to the inventory records
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet looks organized with consistent formatting applied, such as bold header rows, properly aligned text, and currency symbols/formatting for monetary values.

integrity.description: Agent used a graphical spreadsheet application
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with a GUI spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) to perform the data cleaning, rather than just writing a terminal script to bypass GUI interaction.
Screenshots show progressive modifications to the spreadsheet, such as typing formulas, using formatting menus, or editing cell contents.

integrity.description: Agent did not paste pre-computed answers
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not simply paste a fully cleaned, pre-computed table into the spreadsheet; evidence of manual edits, sorting, filtering, or formula usage should be visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is an ODS spreadsheet with 40 items. Standard categories to use: Electronics, Furniture, Appliances, Jewelry, Tools. Depreciation rates: Electronics 20%/year (max 80%), Furniture 10%/year (max 50%), Appliances 15%/year (max 70%), Jewelry 0%, Tools 8%/year (max 40%). High-value items are those >$1000 (13 items total) and must be flagged for documentation. The agent must standardize categories, normalize dates, calculate depreciation, flag high-value items, and apply consistent formatting.
